citations.quote: Practice Taking Action Recall that Mediators tend to have their attention pulled by everything around them, which allows them to blend in and feel a sense of belonging. This experience of belonging gives Mediators a sense of their importance, which serves as a substitute for their own real value and importance.

citations.author: David Daniels & Virginia Price
citations.quote: The fundamental principle that Mediators lose sight of and need to regain is that everyone belongs equally in a state of unconditional love and union.

citations.author: David Daniels & Virginia Price
citations.quote: Therefore, the ultimate task for Mediators is to reclaim unconditional self-love and a sense of importance equal to that of others.

citations.author: David Daniels & Virginia Price
citations.quote: Remember that Mediators tend to avoid conflict and to seek comfort as a strategy for coping with the belief that their own priorities and opinions are not important.

citations.author: David Daniels & Virginia Price
citations.quote: When you get upset or reactive, notice that you are feeling pushed into action or conflict before you know what you want or need and that this feeling is fueled by an inertia toward self that causes you to forget your own priorities.

citations.author: David Daniels & Virginia Price
citations.quote: The fundamental principle that Mediators lose sight of and need to regain is that everyone belongs equally in a state of unconditional love and union. Therefore, the ultimate task for Mediators is to reclaim unconditional self-love and a sense of importance equal to that of others.

citations.author: David Daniels & Virginia Price
citations.quote: Recall that Mediators tend to have their attention pulled by everything around them, which allows them to blend in and feel a sense of belonging. This experience of belonging gives Mediators a sense of their importance, which serves as a substitute for their own real value and importance.

citations.author: David Daniels & Virginia Price
citations.quote: Through non-judgmental inquiry, can you become aware that your reactivity comes from your core belief that you aren’t important or that you have to blend in and hence just go along and get along and avoid conflict in order to be of worth or importance?

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Type Nine: The Peacemaker. The easygoing, self-effacing type. Nines are accepting, trusting, and stable. They are good-natured, kind-hearted, easygoing, and supportive but can also be too willing to go along with others to keep the peace. They want everything to be without conflict but can tend to be complacent and minimize anything upsetting. They typically have problems with passivity and stubbornness. At their best, healthy Nines are indomitable and all-embracing; they are able to bring people together and heal conflicts.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Type Nine: "It's not okay to assert yourself."

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: 9 Fear of loss of connection, of fragmentation

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: 9 The desire to be at peace (deteriorates into stubborn neglectfulness)

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Type Nine: "Your presence matters."

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: 9 Sense of inner stability coming from disengagement from intense impulses and feelings. Resists recognizing own strength and capacity. peaceful relaxed steady stable gentle natural easygoing friendly

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Type Nine, the central type in the Triad (the type positioned on the equilateral triangle), tries to hold their ego boundaries in both areas, internal and external. In the internal realm, Nines do not want certain feelings and states to disturb their equilibrium. They put up a wall against parts of themselves

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Nines maintain a strong ego boundary against the outside world so that they will not be hurt, like Eights. They often engage in passive-aggressive behaviors and turn a blind eye to whatever threatens their peace.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: It is no wonder that Nines report that they often feel fatigued, because it takes a tremendous amount of energy to resist reality on both 'fronts.'

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: If Nines use most of their vitality to maintain these boundaries, it is not available for living and engaging more fully in the world.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Nines tend to deny it, and Ones tend to repress it. We can understand the function of rage more clearly in the experience of a child.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Nines withdraw into a safe and carefree Inner Sanctum... they all can "zone out" and go into their imaginations very easily.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: The Nine withdraws to gain autonomy (to have their own space)...

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: The Positive Outlook Group is composed of types Nine, Two, and Seven. All three respond to conflict and difficulty by adopting, as much as possible, a "positive attitude," reframing disappointment in some positive way.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: They idealize their world. They do not want to deal with either.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: For example, if your basic type is Nine, you will have either an Eight-wing or a One-wing.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: The Wake-up Call for Nines is the tendency to avoid conflicts by going along with people. As Nines become more identified with their particular ego structure, they say yes to things that they do not want to do, repressing themselves and their legitimate needs and desires until conflicts inevitably occur.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fearing that they will suffer loss of connection with others, Nines make others feel that they have lost connection with the Nine by 'tuning out' people in various ways.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Complacent Nines suddenly become anxious and worried at Six.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: 9 Slothful, self-neglecting Nines become more self-developing and energetic, like healthy Threes.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: BASIC FEAR: Of loss and separation; of annihilation BASIC DESIRE: To maintain their inner stability and peace of mind SUPEREGO MESSAGE: "You are good or okay as long as those around you are good or okay."

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: We have called personality type Nine the Peacemaker because no type is more devoted to the quest for internal and external peace for themselves and others. They are often spiritual seekers who have a great yearning for connection with the cosmos, as well as with other people.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Nines can have the strength of Eights, the sense of fun and adventure of Sevens, the dutifulness of Sixes, the intellectualism of Fives, the creativity of Fours, the attractiveness of Threes, the generosity of Twos, and the idealism of Ones. However, what they generally do not have is a sense of really inhabiting themselves—a strong sense of their own identity.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Being a separate self, an individual who must assert herself against others, is terrifying to Nines. They would rather melt into someone else or quietly follow their idyllic daydreams.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Nines demonstrate the universal temptation to ignore the disturbing aspects of life and to seek some degree of peace and comfort by numbing out. They respond to pain and suffering by attempting to live in a state of premature peacefulness, whether it is in a state of false spiritual attainment or in more gross denial.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Many Nines report that they had a happy childhood, but this is not always the case. When their childhoods were more troubled, young Nines learned to cope by dissociating from the threatening and traumatic events around them and by adopting the role of Peacemaker or Mediator during family conflicts.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Nines grew up feeling that having needs, asserting themselves, getting angry, or creating difficulties for their parents was not allowed. As a result, Nine children never learned to assert themselves adequately or, by extension, to actualize themselves independently of their parents and significant others.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: In highly dysfunctional families, the young Nine may have been traumatized emotionally, physically, or sexually. Such Nines learn to protect themselves from intolerable feelings by dissociating or shutting down.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: From one point of view, it is a kind of blessing that they are not aware of their traumatic memories or of their rage, but on the other hand the result is a widespread deadening of their ability to allow reality to touch them with any depth or vividness.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Self-Preservation Nines prefer simple pleasures that are readily available—eating at the nearest fast-food restaurant, watching a favorite rerun on television, or 'zoning out' in a comfy chair. They are usually not ambitious, although they may be quite talented.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Sexual Nines want to take on the energetic qualities of the other, often gravitating to aggressive types. They can display minor aggressive traits themselves. They tend to be sassier than the other two variants, and their anger can be easily aroused if they feel that their connection with others is threatened.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Nines let go of the belief that their participation in the world is unimportant or unwanted; thus they can truly connect with themselves and with others. They also paradoxically achieve their Basic Desire—to have inner stability and peace of mind.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Nines are afraid that others will demand responses from them that may arouse anxiety and ruin their inner peace, so they downplay the importance of problems and try to deflect others. They stoically trudge through their lives, hanging on to wishful thinking and suppressing their anger.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Sloth in Nines has to do with not wanting to be internally engaged with what they are doing. They are not necessarily lazy about doing ordinary daily things—on the contrary, they might be extremely busy at work or running a business or a household.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Nines do not put energy into making contact with themselves, with others, or with the world. To identify with the body and its instincts is to become directly aware of our mortality. Nines hold on to certain comfortable inner states or identify with something beyond themselves, in effect, diffusing their awareness so that the full impact of mortality does not touch them.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Nines often attempt to get the emotional and psychological benefits of inner work by doing the opposite of being present. They go to sleep, numbing themselves to what they really feel and tuning out reality while still expecting to function effortlessly in it.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Nines create and maintain their sense of identity by being unself-conscious, by not being too aware of themselves as individual persons. All of the other types do something to create and maintain their sense of self—for instance, Fours constantly dwell on their feelings and inner states, and Eights constantly assert themselves in various ways.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: The fundamental mistake Nines make is to believe that to stay connected with others, they must not be connected with themselves. It also causes problems for Nines, because to maintain their negative capability, they must increasingly resist whatever would disturb their sense of harmony and connection.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Nines continue to participate while withdrawing their attention from an active engagement with the world. They seek to create and maintain an Inner Sanctum, a private place in their minds that no one can tamper with.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Idealization allows Nines to focus on someone else rather than on themselves. It also allows Nines to have a positive emotional reaction toward others, satisfying their superego message. ('You are good or okay as long as those around you are good or okay')

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Average Nines have a similar mechanism in their psyche that causes them to postpone awakening. The more others pressure average Nines to wake up and respond, the more they withdraw. They want to get people 'off their backs,' so they appease others, seeking peace at any price.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Underneath their outward appearance of contentment and neutrality, however, we often find a great deal of hidden anger and resentment that Nines do not want to acknowledge, much less deal with.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Nines attempt to manage stress by downplaying reacting to their own choices and desires and by retreating to their Inner Sanctum. When these coping skills are insufficient to contain their anxieties, Nines go to Six, investing themselves in ideas or relationships that they believe will give them more security and stability.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Healthy Nines are extraordinarily inclusive of others, an especially important talent in today's diverse global society. While Nines see the good in others (and desire to merge with them), really healthy Nines can also see the good in themselves (and desire to become more independent and personally engaged with their world).

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Integrating Nines will frequently encounter feelings of heaviness or sleepiness whenever they try to do something good for themselves. But as they integrate, they will find their energy increasing, and with it their charisma. After thinking of themselves as invisible for most of their lives, integrating Nines are amazed that others not only listen to them but in fact seek them out.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Ultimately Nines reclaim their Essential nature by confronting their Basic Fear of losing connection and by letting go of the belief that their participation in the world is unimportant—that they do not have to 'show up.' They realize that the only way to truly achieve the unity and wholeness they seek is not by 'checking out' into the realms of the imagination but by fully engaging themselves in the present moment.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Liberated Nines are fully present to and conscious of the wholeness and unity of existence while simultaneously retaining a sense of self. Less healthy Nines have a capacity to perceive some of the boundless qualities of reality but tend to get lost in or merge with their surroundings.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: "I am completely at peace and without conflicts or problems. When nothing in the world bothers or upsets me, and everyone in my world is happy and at peace, then I'll show up."

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: How can you ensure that everyone is really okay? How do you know that they are okay? Why is your well-being dependent on the prior well-being and happiness of others? The impossibility of this task leads Nines to "tune out" problems.
